_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
TCEQ registration and reporting obligations relevant to the workspace fall across three program areas:

**Waste Generator Registration.** Any workspace facility generating Class 1 industrial waste or hazardous waste in Texas must register with TCEQ and obtain a Solid Waste Registration Number. Registered generators and reverse distributors with active solid waste registrations must submit an Industrial & Hazardous Waste Annual Waste Summary (AWS) each year. Facilities that no longer generate or store Class 1 industrial or hazardous waste must formally close all waste management units via documentation submitted to the TCEQ VCP-CA Section (MC-127, PO Box 13087, Austin, TX 78711-3087). Unregistered/inactive generators that need to ship reportable quantities of hazardous or Class 1 waste must submit the Waste Shipment Summary (WSS) form using TCEQ-00757 (Unregistered/Inactive Episodic Generator Notification Form).

**Hazardous Waste Transporter Registration.** If the workspace transports medical waste in Texas, a separate registration is required: submission of Application for Registration by Rule as a Transporter of Medical Waste, form TCEQ-00426. For general hazardous and industrial waste road transport, TCEQ oversight applies under 30 TAC §§335.6(d), 335.11, and 335.91–335.94; a distinct registration pathway applies at a jurisdictional level.

**Air Permit Registration.** For stationary emission sources, the workspace must determine applicability under 30 TAC Chapter 106 (Permits by Rule) or 30 TAC Chapter 116 (New Source Review). Chapter 106 Permits by Rule (PBRs) are available for lower-emission, categorized activities. New Source Review applicants must publish notices and provide plain-language summaries under 30 TAC Chapter 39 Subchapter H, including a Notice of Receipt and Intent to Obtain Permit (NORI) once an application is administratively complete. Specific data fields for permit applications include emission unit descriptions, applicability determinations, and basis for authorization.

**Promised but Not Yet Released.** The proposed rulemaking under Rule Project No. 2026-003-114-AI (Chapter 114 Initial Rental Vehicle Registration Provisions under SB 2102 and SB 1729) has been published for proposal but adoption details and final compliance forms have not yet been released as of the date of this brief. Workspaces with rental or leased vehicles in I/M counties should monitor the TCEQ Rule Projects database for adoption notice.

*Source: Industrial and Hazardous Waste Reporting, TCEQ, March 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/permitting/registration/ihw/waste_reporting.html; Transporting Waste in Texas (RG-086), TCEQ. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/downloads/permitting/waste-registration/pub/rg-086.pdf; Air Permitting Rules and Rulemaking, TCEQ, February 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/permitting/air/rules*
